name: context-attachment-lifecycle
description: End-to-end lifecycle of a context file attachment through the real HTTP + LLM + filesystem stack
given: a fresh barnstormer server with a temp BARNSTORMER_HOME and a configured LLM provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, or Gemini)
when: a user creates a spec with one attached text file, uploads a second file via the brainstorming panel, updates notes, triggers delete, triggers undo, and fetches raw file content
then: the spec is created, both attachments are registered, a real LLM summary is produced and stored for the initial file, notes round-trip through the PATCH endpoint, soft-delete preserves the file on disk, undo restores exactly one entry without duplication, and raw content remains readable across the delete/undo cycle
validates: multipart upload on both POST /web/specs and POST /web/specs/{id}/context
async summarizer calls a real LLM and emits ContextSummarized via the actor
PATCH /web/specs/{id}/context/{att_id}/notes form decoding and state mutation
rendered context panel HTML contains filename, notes, and summary
undo of a ContextRemoved event restores without duplication (regression fix from T4 code review)
GET /raw returns stored content after undo
Askama template renders correctly with nested Option fields
runbook: .scratch/scenario-context-lifecycle.sh (uses PORT=7332 by default to avoid collisions; requires jq + curl + a valid .env)
